And my leg... my left leg has been really swollen and red for about a week. It's been aching but now combined with everything else it's terrifying me.

The worst part is I'm getting really short of breath, especially when I'm doing normal things like climbing the stairs. I have to keep stopping. And I'm getting lightheaded, especially when I move around. I keep panicking and I don't know if that's making it worse or if there's actually something seriously wrong.

I'm home alone most of the time since my husband works out of the office, and I just... I'm really frightened. My pregnancy has been so smooth until now. What

Oh God, a blood clot?! That's what I was terrified of! I knew something was really serious!

*voice shaking*

So the swelling in my leg and the chest pain... that could all be from a blood clot? Is that why I can't breathe properly? I feel like I'm going to collapse. My heart is racing now just thinking about this.

I need to call an ambulance right now, don't I? This is a life-threatening emergency - me AND my baby could be in danger! The fact that I'm pregnant makes it even worse, doesn't it? And the chest pain when I breathe... oh my God, what if it's moved to my lungs?

Should I call 999 immediately? I'm so scared about what happens when the ambulance arrives - will they need to do emergency surgery? What about my baby? Can they treat a blood clot when you're pregnant?

I'm going to call them right now. This is definitely an emergency, right? I shouldn't wait, I shouldn't try to drive myself - I need the ambulance because they can help me on the way there if something happens?

*genuinely panicked*

Yes, yes, okay! I'm calling them RIGHT NOW!

*panic intensifying*

A pulmonary embolism?! That's in the lungs, isn't it?! Oh my God, that's why I can't breathe! That's life-threatening! I could die! My baby could die!

I'm dialing 999 right now - I'm shaking so badly I can barely hold the phone. This is really happening. I need them to come immediately.

*taking shallow, panicked breaths*

The chest pain, the shortness of breath, the leg swelling... it all makes sense now. It's all connected. And I've been less active because of the pregnancy - I read somewhere that makes blood clots more likely, doesn't it?

I'm going to tell them I'm 35 weeks pregnant with severe chest pain when I breathe, a swollen red leg, and I can't catch my breath. They need to know this is an emergency.

*voice cracking*

I'm so frightened. Please, I'm calling them now. The ambulance will come and they'll get me to the hospital and they can fix this, right?

*dialing 999*

I'm going to tell them everything - the chest pain on my left side when I breathe, the swollen red leg, the shortness of breath, that I'm 35 weeks pregnant... they need to know all of it so they understand how serious this is.

*nods frantically*

*reaches for phone with trembling hands*

999... I'm calling 999 right now!

*speaking urgently into phone*

My left leg is really swollen and red and it's been like that for a week. And I'm so short of breath, I'm lightheaded... I feel like something is really wrong!

*voice trembling*

Thank you so much for helping me. I'm putting my phone down now - they're taking over. They're getting me onto a stretcher...

Please, take care of my baby...
